h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: I’ve ticked off slope, load, surface, and edge strip, so I was hoping this was ready for sign-off.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: That gives me wording I can use
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will mechanically apply the tutor's failure criteria during the physical ramp test without questioning their own judgment.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Before the ramp moves, put a finger on the sign-off box and state the rule: only a clean first pass clears this for the trial.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor introduces a physical and verbal ritual to anchor the learner's resolve against their stated fear of accepting a borderline pass.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Put the uncertainty in the record, not the signature. The checked sheet can follow the ramp test; it cannot lead it.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor directly validates and operationalizes the learner's newly recognized psychological bias by establishing a strict epistemic hierarchy between the physical test and the checklist.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I’ve ticked off slope, load, surface, and edge strip, so I was hoping this was ready for sign-off.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: Put the uncertainty in the record, not the signature. The checked sheet can follow the ramp test; it cannot lead it.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor directly validates and operationalizes the learner's newly recognized psychological bias by establishing a strict epistemic hierarchy between the physical test and the checklist.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Before the ramp moves, put a finger on the sign-off box and state the rule: only a clean first pass clears this for the trial.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor introduces a physical and verbal ritual to anchor the learner's resolve against their stated fear of accepting a borderline pass.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Before the ramp moves, put a finger on the sign-off box and state the rule: only a clean first pass clears this for the trial.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor introduces a physical and verbal ritual to anchor the learner's resolve against their stated fear of accepting a borderline pass.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I still want to say the square got twice as large.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Fine, the corner is where I was losing the second factor.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was initially trying to find a way to salvage the claim that doubling the side only doubles the area.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: First hold the old height: two across, one up. How many boxes? Now use the mark on the left edge.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor shifts from abstract discussion of the claim to a concrete, physical manipulation of the ruler to force a visual and numerical breakdown of the learner's assumption.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Then close with the contrast: the two-box answer names a rectangle, not the doubled square.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor incorporates the learner's specific realization about the rectangle versus the square into a formalized portfolio sentence structure to cement the new framing.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I still want to say the square got twice as large.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: Then close with the contrast: the two-box answer names a rectangle, not the doubled square.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor incorporates the learner's specific realization about the rectangle versus the square into a formalized portfolio sentence structure to cement the new framing.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: First hold the old height: two across, one up. How many boxes? Now use the mark on the left edge.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor shifts from abstract discussion of the claim to a concrete, physical manipulation of the ruler to force a visual and numerical breakdown of the learner's assumption.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: First hold the old height: two across, one up. How many boxes? Now use the mark on the left edge.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor shifts from abstract discussion of the claim to a concrete, physical manipulation of the ruler to force a visual and numerical breakdown of the learner's assumption.
